title: Mexican Pork Ribs
description: A delicious and tender Mexican-inspired pork ribs recipe, perfect for a special occasion or a weekend barbecue. The combination of spices, herbs, and a sweet and tangy BBQ sauce makes this dish a standout.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together cumin, chili powder, oregano, lime juice, and brown sugar.
Add the pork ribs to the marinade and toss to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Remove the ribs from the marinade and place them on a large baking sheet lined with aluminum foil.
Drizzle the ribs with olive oil and rub them all over with your hands to coat evenly.
Cover the baking sheet with foil and bake the ribs for 2 hours.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes, or until the ribs are nicely browned.
Transfer the ribs to a grill or grill pan and cook for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until nicely charred.
Remove the ribs from the heat and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
